mission.title: Install and Configure Argo Events (Argo) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Argo Events is an event-driven workflow automation framework for Kubernetes that allows you to trigger various actions based on multiple event sources. Installing Argo Events enables you to automate workflows in response to events, enhancing your Kubernetes applications' responsiveness and interactivity.

steps.title: Add the Argo Events Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Argo Events Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the charts needed for installation.
```bash
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Argo Events using Helm
steps.description:
Next, install Argo Events in a new namespace called `argo-events` using the specified version.
```bash
helm install argo-events argo/argo-events --namespace argo-events --create-namespace --version v1.9.10
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Argo Events components are running successfully by listing the pods in the `argo-events` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Argo Events
steps.description:
Create a ServiceAccount and ClusterRoleBinding for Argo Events to ensure it has the necessary permissions.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook-sa
  namespace: argo-events
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["argoproj.io"]
  resources: ["eventbus", "eventsources", "sensors"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Argo Events webhook
steps.description:
To access the Argo Events webhook service, set up port forwarding. This allows you to interact with the service locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/events-webhook -n argo-events 8080:443
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Argo Events will show all pods in the `argo-events` namespace in a running state. You can also access the webhook service locally via port forwarding, enabling you to test event sources.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
helm install argo-events argo/argo-events --namespace argo-events --create-namespace --version v1.9.10

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook-sa
  namespace: argo-events
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["argoproj.io"]
  resources: ["eventbus", "eventsources", "sensors"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
